meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your SaaS development.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you weaponize AI-driven Git workflows. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refactoring, you can transform abstract ideas into production-ready repositories in hours, not weeks. This approach creates high-fidelity proof-of-work that demonstrates technical execution to investors and customers alike.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You have the vision for a SaaS product, but the market demands proof—a portfolio of shipped, functional code that separates the dreamers from the founders. If you lack the history of deployments, you are effectively invisible in an ecosystem that values execution over intent.

But the rules of the game have changed. By integrating AI into your Git workflow, you are no longer just a developer; you are an architect orchestrating intelligence. This tutorial strips away the manual friction of coding, allowing you to build, commit, and deploy high-value features that command attention and validate your business model.

steps.title: Architecting the MVP Foundation
steps.description: Define the scope of your SaaS architecture to ensure the code you write is modular and scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Design a directory structure for a high-performance Next.js SaaS MVP that includes authentication, database integration, and Stripe billing. Output the project tree and configuration files.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding based on the architectural design provided.'

steps.title: Automating Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to translate business requirements into functional endpoints without getting bogged down in boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust API endpoint for user onboarding that handles edge cases and integrates with a mock database provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the user onboarding API endpoint with Zod validation and error handling.'

steps.title: Strategic Git Commit Hygiene
steps.description: Maintain a pristine commit history that tells a story of iterative growth, which is essential for impressing potential stakeholders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Git hook script that enforces semantic commit messages and prevents pushing code that lacks unit test coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure git hooks and verify the pre-commit workflow for semantic consistency.'

steps.title: Automated Testing for Credibility
steps.description: Your portfolio is only as strong as your testing suite. AI can generate tests that prove your code is battle-tested.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of integration tests for the authentication flow using Playwright, focusing on critical paths.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write and execute integration tests for the auth module to ensure 90% coverage.'

steps.title: Documentation and Deployment
steps.description: Finalize your repo with a high-conversion README that highlights the business logic and technical stack used.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md that explains the project's business impact, architecture choices, and how to deploy to production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate documentation and prepare the deployment configuration files for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Treat your commit messages as marketing copy; investors look at your Git history to gauge your attention to detail.
Use Claude Code to 'code review' your own PRs before merging to identify architectural bottlenecks early.
Always include a 'Business Logic' section in your repo to prove you understand the problem, not just the syntax.
Automate your CI/CD pipeline early; a green checkmark on your repo is a visual indicator of a professional-grade product.
Focus on 'feature density'—fewer features, perfectly executed, look better than a bloated codebase that doesn't solve a specific problem.
resources: Git Best Practices for Founders: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your repository is polished, deploy it to a live environment, link the URL in your README, and begin shipping updates to start gathering real-world user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build a portfolio that commands authority and secures revenue. Don't let the 'no portfolio' label hold back your potential as a founder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
